mustache-desc: Allows trusted users to create raw HTML snippets and mustache templates that can be used on content pages with the {{#mustache:}} and {{#html:}} parser functions.
mustache-error-template-not-found: Mustache template not found or empty: [[$1]]
mustache-error-wrong-content-model: Mustache template [[$1]] has the wrong content model
mustache-error-raw-interpolation: Raw interpolation using {{{ or {{& and Set Delimiter tag {{= are not allowed in Mustache templates for security reasons.
mustache-error-script-interpolation: Mustache interpolation inside <script> tags requires a JavaScript-safe filter: js-identifier or js-string.
mustache-error-style-interpolation: Mustache interpolation inside <style> tags requires a CSS-safe filter: css-selector or css-value.
mustache-error-unknown-filter: Unknown Mustache filter "$1". Allowed filters: css-selector, css-value, js-string, js-identifier, url, and attribute.
mustache-error-attribute-filter-required: The $1 attribute in tag $2 must use the $3 filter when using interpolation.
mustache-error-url-filter-required: The $1 attribute in tag $2 must have a url filter if it starts with an interpolation.
mustache-error-dangerous-attribute: Mustache syntax is not permitted in attribute "$1" of tag "$2" for security reasons. If you need to customize this attribute, use data-* attributes to pass data and insert attributes with JS instead.
mustache-error-attribute-name-interpolat: Mustache syntax is not permitted in attribute names of tag "$1".
mustache-error-permission-denied: You need both editsitecss and editsitejs rights to edit mustache templates or raw html.
mustache-import-error-invalid-content: An imported Mustache template contains invalid content.
